sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Excellent! New findings:
1. From [39,43], DOWN → [30,31] cols [39,43] (as expected)
2. From [30,31] cols [39,43], LEFT → [30,31] cols [34,38] (NEW! This is a direct connection!)
3. From [30,31] cols [34,38], UP → [25,26] cols [34,38]
4. From [25,26] cols [34,38], DOWN → [30,31] cols [34,38]
5. From [30,31] cols [34,38], RIGHT → [39,43]
So the corridor at rows [30,31] connects cols [34,38] and cols [39,43]. This is a horizontal corridor at rows [30,31].
Now I have a clearer picture:
- Vertical corridor at cols [34,38]: rows [20,21] to [40,41]
- Vertical corridor at cols [39,43]: rows [25,26] to [30,31]
- Horizontal corridor at rows [30,31]: cols [34,38] to [39,43]
- Horizontal corridor at rows [25,26]: cols [34,38] to [39,43]
Wait, but earlier I observed that from [25,26] cols [34,38], RIGHT → [39,43]. And from [39,43], UP → [25,26] cols [39,43]. So there's also a horizontal connection at rows [25,26].
But from [25,26] cols [39,43], DOWN → [30,31] cols [39,43]. And from [30,31] cols [39,43], LEFT → [30,31] cols [34,38]. So there's a horizontal connection at rows [30,31] as well.
So the maze has:
- Vertical corridor at cols [34,38]: rows [20,21] to [40,41]
- Vertical corridor at cols [39,43]: rows [25,26] to [30,31]
- Horizontal corridor at rows [25,26]: cols [34,38] to [39,43]
- Horizontal corridor at rows [30,31]: cols [34,38] to [39,43]
This forms a rectangular loop:
- [20,21] cols [34,38] → DOWN → [25,26] cols [34,38] → RIGHT → [39,43] → DOWN → [30,31] cols [39,43] → LEFT → [34,38] → UP → [25,26] cols [34,38]
But I still can't reach the right room. The white cross is at rows [46,47] cols [51,52].
Wait, let me look at the boundary again for the right room:
- [39, 54] to [39, 58]: top
- [39, 58] to [54, 58]: right
- [54, 58] to [54, 39]: bottom
- [54, 39] to [50, 39]: bottom-left
- [50, 39] to [50, 43]: left going up
- [50, 43] to [49, 44]: diagonal
- [49, 44] to [40, 44]: left going up
- [40, 44] to [40, 48]: top-left
- [40, 48] to [39, 49]: diagonal
- [39, 49] to [20, 49]: left going up
The right room has an opening at row 40, cols [44,48]. The blocks at rows [30,31] cols [39,43] are above this opening. To reach the opening, the blocks need to move DOWN from rows [30,31] to rows [39,43] or [40,44].
But from [39,43], DOWN → [30,31] cols [34,38] (not further down). This suggests the corridor at cols [39,43] ends at rows [30,31].
Wait, but the boundary shows [39, 49] to [20, 49]: vertical at col 49, rows 20-39. This is the left side of the right room. The blocks at cols [39,43] are adjacent to col 49. If the blocks could move RIGHT from cols [39,43] to cols [44,48], they would enter the right room. But RIGHT is blocked.
Hmm, maybe the issue is that the blocks are 5x5, and the opening is at row 40, cols [44,48]. The blocks need to be at rows [40,44] to enter this opening. But the blocks are currently at rows [30,31], which is above row 40.
Let me try: from [30,31] cols [39,43], go DOWN to see if the blocks can reach rows [40,44].
Wait, earlier I observed that from [39,43], DOWN → [30,31] cols [34,38]. But the blocks are currently at [30,31] cols [39,43]. Let me try DOWN from here.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: rows [30,31] cols [39,43]
# From [30,31] cols [39,43], LEFT → [34,38] (discovered)
# Let me try DOWN from [39,43] to see if blocks can reach the right room

predict(expect_change=True, note='DOWN from [39,43]')
action(['DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Orange: rows [{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols [{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

# Try RIGHT
predict(expect_change=True, note='RIGHT')
action(['RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Orange: rows [{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols [{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

# Try DOWN
predict(expect_change=True, note='DOWN')
action(['DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Orange: rows [{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols [{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

# Try RIGHT
predict(expect_change=True, note='RIGHT')
action(['RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Orange: rows [{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols [{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
